title: The First Tome: On the First Death
lore: Nothing here dies only once. The first creature to fall learned that the dark returns what it takes — smaller, hungrier, wiser. They named this turning the cycle of rebirth.
deep: Each rebirth strips the flesh but leaves a scar of memory in the soul; the System reads that scar to decide what you may become.

title: On Wounds and Mending
lore: Flesh torn in the dark does not wait for kindness; survivors learn to knit themselves shut between heartbeats.
deep: Regeneration borrows from tomorrow's strength — push it too far and the body forgets how to stop closing.

title: The Weaver's Patience
lore: The strongest hunter never chases; it waits at the center of a trap spun in silence.
deep: Mastery is not in the strike but in the stillness that makes the strike unnecessary.

title: Hunger, the Silent Court
lore: The dungeon judges no one; only hunger holds court here, and it taxes the patient and the proud alike in flesh.
deep: To eat your own kind quiets the hunger but stains the ruler within — gluttony is the throne hunger offers.

title: Hatchling's First Thread
lore: A newborn weaver wakes already hunted; its first thread is not a home but a noose for something smaller.
deep: Silk remembers every tension it has ever held — it is the spider's oldest memory.

title: The Many Eyes
lore: Eight eyes do not see eight worlds; they see one world eight times, and the spider learns which lie to trust.
deep: Appraisal was born here — the habit of looking until the truth has nowhere left to hide.

title: Venom and Silk
lore: One end of the spider kills, the other binds; mastery is knowing which the moment demands.
deep: Venom is impatience made liquid; silk is patience made solid.

title: The Labyrinth Within
lore: A spider that survives long enough stops fearing the maze and becomes it.
deep: They whisper of one who mapped the whole labyrinth in her mind and was never lost again.

title: The Weaver's Fear
lore: Even the patient hunter dreads the open floor, where nothing waits above to drop from.
deep: A spider's courage is measured in ceilings.

title: Arachne Ascendant
lore: The final weaver no longer waits at the center of her web — she is the web, and the dungeon is caught in it.
deep: To become Arachne is to stop catching prey and start catching fates.

title: The First Droplet
lore: The weakest thing in the dark is a slime — and it survives longest, because nothing bothers to fear it.
deep: Being underestimated is the slime's first and best armor.

title: The Formless Body
lore: Having no shape, the slime cannot break the way shaped things break; it only divides and reconvenes.
deep: A wound to a slime is just a new edge to flow around.

title: Predator's Hunger
lore: The slime does not eat to fill itself; it eats to become what it ate.
deep: Every meal is a question — what of you is worth keeping?

title: The Name
lore: A slime given a true name stops being a monster and starts being a person — and then a sovereign.
deep: The naming is not a gift from outside; it is the moment the slime decides who it is.

title: The Fragile Core
lore: Boundless as its body is, a slime is only ever one small core; pierce it and all that formlessness cannot save it.
deep: The strongest survivor hides the smallest secret.

title: Sovereign of Slimes
lore: The apex slime no longer mimics what it eats — the world begins to mimic it.
deep: Formlessness, taken far enough, becomes the freedom to be every form at once.

title: The Weakest Beginning
lore: A human is born with no fang, no scale, no venom — only the stubborn habit of refusing to die.
deep: Weakness that survives becomes the most dangerous thing of all.

title: Body of Balance
lore: Where beasts specialize, the human spreads thin and calls it versatility.
deep: To be good at everything is to be free to become anything.

title: The Path Chosen
lore: Every human reaches a fork — shield, blade, spell, or mercy — and the dungeon remembers the choice.
deep: The path does not reveal who you are; it decides it.

title: Sword and Spell
lore: The human who refuses to choose between steel and sorcery is rare, and rarely lives — but when one does, kingdoms kneel.
deep: Mastery of two arts is not twice the power; it is a third art no one else can name.

title: Mortality's Curse
lore: The human fears death more than any beast, and that fear is the engine of every cathedral and every crime.
deep: Knowing you will end is the only reason to build something that won't.

title: The Crowned
lore: The apex human is not the strongest body but the will that bends others to a shared dream.
deep: A sovereign is a story so many believe that it becomes true.

title: Risen from Bone
lore: Death was meant to be the end; for the skeleton it was merely a door that opened the wrong way.
deep: What rises remembers dying, and that memory is sharper than any blade.

title: The Hollow Frame
lore: A skeleton carries no flesh to bleed, no organ to fail — only the architecture of what it was.
deep: Emptiness is not weakness; it is nothing left for pain to grip.

title: Undying Marrow
lore: Shatter a skeleton and the pieces still itch to rejoin; the dead do not accept dismissal.
deep: Regeneration without flesh is pure will wearing the shape of bone.

title: The Lich's Bargain
lore: A skeleton that binds its own soul into a vessel buys eternity at the price of warmth.
deep: The lich does not fear death, for it has already paid death in full.

title: The Brittle Truth
lore: For all its defiance, bone is dry and snaps; the undead's strength is also its oldest flaw.
deep: To endure forever is to crack forever — the lich's true labor is mending.

title: Death Sovereign
lore: The apex undead no longer flees death — it commands it, and the dungeon's dead answer.
deep: To rule death is to make every ending a beginning you authored.

title: The Hatchling Drake
lore: A wyrmling breaks its shell already arrogant, for it remembers, dimly, that its kin once ruled the sky.
deep: Pride is the dragon's first scale and its last weakness.

title: Scales and Flame
lore: The drake's body is a forge; what it eats becomes heat, and what angers it becomes fire.
deep: A dragon's temper is not a flaw in its power — it is the fuel.

title: The Hoard Instinct
lore: Long before it can breathe fire, a drake begins to count what is its, and to resent what is not.
deep: Greed in a dragon is not vice but instinct — the world is a hoard it has not yet claimed.

title: Breath of Ruin
lore: The first true breath is the moment a drake stops being prey and the dungeon learns to flinch.
deep: Fire is the simplest argument; few survive to disagree.

title: The Long Slumber
lore: A dragon's quiet enemy is time and the sloth it breeds; many an elder wyrm slept until it was forgotten.
deep: Power that rests too long is indistinguishable from power that is gone.

title: Elder Dragon
lore: The apex wyrm is a force the dungeon plans around, like weather or grief.
deep: An elder dragon does not win battles; it ends the era in which battles were possible.

title: The First Pebble
lore: A golem begins as a stone that refused to stay still, woken by a rune no one remembers carving.
deep: The smallest stone that thinks is already more than the mountain that does not.

title: Body of Stone
lore: The golem feels no hunger, no fatigue, no fear of bleeding — it has traded speed for the patience of geology.
deep: To a golem, a siege is a brief inconvenience and a century is an afternoon.

title: The Core Rune
lore: Every golem is one carved word holding a body together; erase the word and the body remembers it is only rubble.
deep: A golem's soul is grammar — a single law that stone agrees to obey.

title: Unyielding
lore: Where other races starve, the golem simply continues; it is the dungeon's most honest survivor.
deep: Needing nothing is the deepest kind of freedom, and the loneliest.

title: The Crack
lore: Stone that cannot bend must break; the golem's strength hides a brittleness no flesh would tolerate.
deep: The unyielding thing fears only the flaw it cannot feel forming.

title: World-Golem
lore: The apex golem stops being a thing in the world and becomes a piece of its bedrock.
deep: Grown large enough, the difference between a titan and a landscape disappears.
